title: feat(studio): AI-powered BigQueryâ†’ClickHouse logs query rewrite
overview: This PR adds an AI-powered query rewrite flow for the Logs Explorer so that users' saved BigQuery-dialect queries continue to work after the backend moved to ClickHouse. The core new file is `logs-sql-rewrite.ts`, which builds a detailed prompt and calls the existing `/api/ai/code/complete` endpoint with a new `dialect: 'clickhouse'` flag. The API endpoint was updated to branch on that flag, skipping the Postgres schema fetch and using a dedicated ClickHouse system prompt and model. The Explorer page gains a banner (shown once, dismissal persisted) and a toolbar button that trigger the rewrite, then overlay a Monaco diff editor for accept/discard review. A parallel new file (`Logs.fieldReference.ts`) maps BigQuery-style field schemas to OTEL `log_attributes` paths, and a new data query (`otel-log-keys-query.ts`) discovers real `log_attributes` keys from live data so the prompt and Field Reference panel show exact paths. Read from the API contract inward: endpoint â†’ rewrite logic â†’ data layer â†’ UI entry points â†’ field reference â†’ tests.

chapters.title: POST /api/ai/code/complete â€” ClickHouse dialect branch
chapters.summary: The completion API gains a `dialect` field; when set to `clickhouse` it bypasses the Postgres schema fetch, uses a dedicated model and system prompt, and sends the rewrite prompt directly as the user message.

chapters.title: AI rewrite logic â€” prompt, detection, and network call
chapters.summary: New `logs-sql-rewrite.ts` encapsulates everything the client needs to invoke the rewrite: detecting whether a query is legacy BigQuery, identifying its source, building the ClickHouse conversion prompt, and posting to the endpoint.

chapters.title: Live log_attributes key discovery â€” otel-log-keys-query
chapters.summary: New data-layer module queries real ClickHouse data to discover which `log_attributes` keys a source actually emits, so both the rewrite prompt and the Field Reference panel show exact paths.

chapters.title: Logs Explorer page â€” rewrite entry points, diff review overlay, and OTEL source insertion
chapters.summary: The Explorer page wires together detection, the banner, the toolbar button, the AI call, and the Monaco diff overlay for accept/discard.

chapters.title: OTEL banner and 'Fix Query' toolbar button components
chapters.summary: Two new/modified UI components provide the visible entry points for the rewrite flow.

chapters.title: OTEL field schema mapping â€” Logs.fieldReference.ts
chapters.summary: A new module converts the existing BigQuery-style log field schemas (from `shared-data`) into OTEL-schema representations with `log_attributes['key']` paths, and builds field lists from dynamically discovered keys.

chapters.title: LogsExplorerHeader â€” OTEL-aware Field Reference
chapters.summary: The header's Field Reference drawer gains the same OTEL-mode behaviour as the panel version: schema transformation, discovered-key loading, and dialect-appropriate description text.

chapters.title: useLogsUrlState â€” stable setter callbacks
chapters.summary: All setter functions in the URL-state hook are wrapped in `useCallback` so their identities are stable across renders, preventing spurious re-executions of effects that depend on them.

chapters.title: Tests
chapters.summary: Unit tests cover every new pure function and the network call.

units.summary: Adds `dialect: 'clickhouse'` to the request schema and derives `isClickhouse` from it.

units.layerReason: Model selection at the API layer
units.summary: Introduces a separate model entry (`gpt-5.4-nano` with `reasoningEffort: 'low'`) used only for log rewrites, selected when `isClickhouse` is true.
New system-prompt fragment that tells the model this is ClickHouse (not Postgres/BigQuery): single `logs` table, `log_attributes` map lookups, ClickHouse function names.

units.symbol: handler â€” clickhouse vs postgres branching
units.summary: For ClickHouse: skips schema fetch (`includeSchema = false`), builds a minimal system prompt from `CLICKHOUSE_LOGS_COMPLETION_INSTRUCTIONS`, and sends the rewrite prompt as the raw user message instead of wrapping it in a schema-annotated template.

`detectLogSource` extracts the log source from either a `source = '...'` filter or a legacy `FROM <table>` clause (with alias normalisation for `pg_cron_logs`). `looksLikeLegacyLogsQuery` uses heuristics (unnest, cast-timestamp, non-`logs` FROM) to decide whether the banner/button should appear.

The schema reference constant teaches the model the ClickHouse `logs` table shape. `renderAvailableKeys` injects real discovered keys so the model uses exact dotted paths rather than guessing. The 6-rule prompt + worked example gives the model unambiguous conversion instructions.
Strips markdown code fences from the model response in case the model wraps output despite being told not to.

units.layerReason: Network call, depends on prompt builder and endpoint above
units.summary: Posts to `/api/ai/code/complete` with `dialect: 'clickhouse'`, the full prompt, and the original SQL as `selection`. Throws on HTTP error or empty result after stripping fences.

Runs `SELECT arrayJoin(mapKeys(log_attributes)) ... FROM logs WHERE source = ?` over the last 7 days, returning keys sorted by frequency. Uses parameterised `safeSql`/`analyticsLiteral` to avoid injection.
React Query wrapper around `fetchOtelLogKeys` with a 5-minute stale time; only fires when both `projectRef` and `source` are present and `enabled` is true.
Imports `rewriteLogsSqlWithAI`, `detectLogSource`, `looksLikeLegacyLogsQuery`, `DiffEditor`, `fetchOtelLogKeys`, project/org hooks, and defines `otelSourceQuery` for OTEL-aware source insertion.

Adds `rewriteProposal` (original/modified pair), `isRewriting`, and `rewriteBannerDismissed` (persisted via localStorage). `showRewriteCTA` gates both entry points: only shown when the OTEL endpoint is active and the current query looks legacy.

units.layerReason: Primary orchestration logic for the feature
units.summary: `handleRewrite` detects the source, fetches real keys, calls `rewriteLogsSqlWithAI`, then guards against clobbering an editor that changed while the async calls were in flight before setting `rewriteProposal`. Accept writes the result into the editor; discard clears the proposal.

When the OTEL endpoint is active, 'Insert source' either replaces an existing `source = '...'` filter in-place or replaces the whole query with the OTEL starter template, instead of the old string-splice into `from`.
Collapses the two separate useEffects (one for search/q, one for OTEL placeholder) into one. The OTEL vs. legacy placeholder choice is resolved upstream via `PLACEHOLDER_QUERY`.

units.symbol: banner, toolbar button, and diff overlay in JSX
units.summary: Renders `LogsExplorerOtelBanner` (below the query panel, shown until dismissed), passes `showRewriteAction`/`isRewriting`/`onRewrite` to `LogsQueryPanel` (shown after banner is dismissed), and overlays a `DiffEditor` with Accept/Discard controls when a `rewriteProposal` is pending.

units.layerReason: UI component, consumer of parent state
units.summary: An `Admonition`-based banner with 'Rewrite with Assistant' and 'Dismiss' actions. Shown only until dismissed; dismissal is persisted so it doesn't reappear.

units.symbol: LogsQueryPanel â€” new props and imports
units.summary: Adds `showRewriteAction`, `isRewriting`, `onRewrite` props; switches from `SidePanel` to `Sheet` for the Field Reference drawer; imports OTEL field utilities and the new log-keys query.

Under the `otelLegacyLogs` flag, applies `toOtelFieldSchemas` to convert BigQuery-style field paths to `log_attributes[...]` form, and fetches real discovered keys for the selected source when the drawer is open.

units.symbol: LogsQueryPanel â€” Field Reference UI overhaul
units.layerReason: UI restructuring and OTEL-aware field rendering
units.summary: Replaces the old `SidePanel`/`Table` (to-be-cleaned) with a `Sheet`/shadcn `Table`. Adds the 'Rewrite query to ClickHouse SQL' tooltip+button next to Field Reference. In OTEL mode, shows a loading skeleton while keys are fetched, then renders `otelFieldsFromKeys(discoveredKeys)` instead of the static schema.

units.layerReason: Visual fix in the same area
units.summary: Minor padding tweak to the error code block.
Defines the five fixed columns in the ClickHouse `logs` table (`id`, `timestamp`, `event_message`, `severity_text`, `source`) that are real columns, not `log_attributes` entries.

`toOtelAttributeKey` strips the `metadata.` prefix; `toLogAttributePath` produces the `log_attributes['...']` syntax with escaping. `toOtelFieldSchema` keeps the base columns unchanged and remaps every other field through those two helpers.

units.layerReason: Domain utility composing base fields + discovered keys
units.summary: Produces a field list from dynamically discovered key strings (returned by `fetchOtelLogKeys`), filtering out keys that are already real columns to avoid duplicates.
Imports the field reference utilities and the log-keys query. Under the flag, applies `toOtelFieldSchemas` and fetches `discoveredKeys` for the selected source when the panel is open.
Branches the description text (unnesting joins vs. `log_attributes['key']` map lookups) and the field table body (`otelFieldsFromKeys(discoveredKeys)` vs. static schema fields), with a loading state.

units.layerReason: Hook behaviour fix; enables the Explorer page effect change
units.summary: Stabilising these callbacks is a prerequisite for including them in the simplified `useEffect` dependency array in the Explorer page without triggering infinite loops.

Tests `buildClickhouseRewritePrompt` (schema reference inclusion, key injection, worked example), `detectLogSource` (both path styles, alias mapping), `looksLikeLegacyLogsQuery` (positive and negative cases), `stripSqlCodeFences`, and `rewriteLogsSqlWithAI` (happy path, HTTP error, empty response).

Tests `toOtelFieldSchemas` (base columns preserved, non-column fields remapped, `metadata.` prefix dropped, types preserved) and `otelFieldsFromKeys` (base fields, key-to-path mapping, column exclusion, escaping, empty input).
Updates the import to use the now-named export `LogsQueryPanel`.
